cases.name: URGENT: Overdue Payment - Crest Electrical Invoice #4892
cases.description: Severely overdue invoice from Crest Electrical for emergency lighting work at Graylings (€3,240 + VAT). Originally 19 days overdue on March 5th, now 61+ days overdue. Contractor threatening work hold affecting 2 Citynorth Quarter jobs. Immediate payment required to maintain contractor relationship.

cases.name: EMERGENCY: Fire Safety Non-Compliance - Citynorth Quarter
cases.description: Dublin Fire Brigade inspection found 4 non-compliant fire safety issues at Citynorth Quarter. Remediation deadline March 27th MISSED by 19 days. Issues: faulty fire doors (floors 3,7,10), blocked escape stairwell, missing bin store suppression, inadequate emergency lighting. Risk of enforcement action/closure orders.

cases.name: EMERGENCY: Heating Failure - Infant Welfare & Legal Threat
cases.description: Serious heating failure at Reds Works property with infant present. Threshold Housing Advice CC-d management company advising tenant Grainne about 3-day heating outage. Threshold recommending RTB dispute and local authority complaint. Urgent response required - child welfare and legal compliance at risk.

emails.subject: Overdue Invoice #4892 - Graylings emergency lighting
emails.body:
Hi,

This is a follow-up regarding Invoice #4892 for the emergency lighting replacement works completed at Graylings on January 15th, 2026.

Invoice amount: €3,240.00 + VAT
Payment terms: 30 days
Due date: February 14th, 2026
Days overdue: 19 days

We completed this work on an emergency basis over a weekend at your request. We've sent two reminders already (Feb 18th and Feb 28th) with no response.

Please arrange payment within 7 days or we'll need to put a hold on any further work at your properties until the account is cleared. We have two jobs scheduled at citynorth Quarter next week that would be affected.

Kind regards,
Mairead Coyne
Accounts Department
Crest Electrical Ltd

emails.subject: Re: URGENT - Payment Processing Invoice #4892
emails.body:
Dear Mairead,

We sincerely apologise for the unacceptable delay in payment of Invoice #4892 for the emergency lighting work at Graylings.

We acknowledge that this invoice is now significantly overdue and we take full responsibility for this oversight. Your emergency work was completed professionally and on time, and you deserve prompt payment.

**IMMEDIATE ACTION:**
- Payment of €3,240.00 + VAT is being processed today
- You will receive payment confirmation within 24 hours
- We will call you this afternoon to confirm details

**Request:**
We urgently request that you proceed with the scheduled work at Citynorth Quarter as planned. These jobs are critical for fire safety compliance.

We are implementing immediate changes to our accounts payable procedures to prevent future delays with your invoices.

Thank you for your patience and continued professional service.

Urgent regards,

Accounts Department
ManageCo Property Management

emails.subject: Re: URGENT - Fire Safety Remediation Plan - Citynorth Quarter
emails.body:
Dear Dublin Fire Brigade Fire Prevention Section,

We acknowledge receipt of the fire safety inspection report for Citynorth Quarter dated March 5th, 2026.

We sincerely apologise for our delayed response to your remediation requirements. This delay is unacceptable given the critical nature of fire safety compliance.

**IMMEDIATE REMEDIATION PLAN:**

**Week 1 (Immediate Actions):**
- Fire doors Floors 3, 7, 10: Emergency contractor engaged, repairs within 48 hours
- Fire escape stairwell Floor 4: Items removed immediately, compliance signage installed
- Emergency lighting car park: Contractor testing and battery replacement within 3 days

**Week 2-3:**
- Bin store fire suppression system: Contractors engaged, installation scheduling (requires planning approval)

**Completion Timeline:**
All items except bin store suppression: Completed by April 22nd
Bin store suppression system: Completed by May 6th (pending approvals)

**Immediate Contact:**
We will call your office today to discuss this timeline and any enforcement implications.

We take full responsibility for this delay and are implementing enhanced compliance monitoring to prevent future occurrences.

Urgent regards,

Sarah Brennan
Fire Safety Compliance Manager
Citynorth Quarter Management

emails.subject: Fire safety inspection report - citynorth Quarter
emails.body:
Dear Ms. Brennan,

Please find attached the fire safety inspection report for citynorth Quarter following our inspection on February 27th, 2026.

Key findings:

COMPLIANT:
- Fire detection and alarm system
- Emergency lighting (common areas)
- Fire extinguisher locations and servicing
- Evacuation signage
- Fire safety register (mostly complete)

NON-COMPLIANT (must be addressed within 28 days):
1. Fire doors on Floors 3, 7, and 10 — door closers not functioning correctly
2. Fire escape stairwell Floor 4 — items stored in stairwell (fire hazard)
3. Bin store — no automatic fire suppression system (required for enclosed bin stores in multi-storey residential)
4. Emergency lighting battery backup in car park — below minimum duration

RECOMMENDATIONS:
- Upgrade fire door inspection regime to quarterly
- Install additional smoke detection in car park areas
- Review fire evacuation procedure for mobility-impaired residents

Please provide a remediation plan by March 27th, 2026.

Dublin Fire Brigade, Fire Prevention Section

emails.subject: Re: Your query - Heating obligation
emails.body:
Dear Grainne,

Thank you for contacting Threshold. We've reviewed the details you've provided regarding the heating failure at your apartment.

Under Section 12(1)(b) of the Residential Tenancies Act 2004, your landlord is obligated to maintain the property in a proper state of repair. The Housing (Standards for Rented Houses) Regulations 2019 specifically require that heating facilities are maintained in good repair and working order.

A heating failure lasting 3 days, particularly with a young infant in the property, is a serious breach. We recommend:

1. Send a written notice to your landlord/agent by email (for a paper trail) detailing the issue, the dates reported, and the lack of response
2. Set a reasonable deadline (48 hours given the circumstances)
3. If not resolved, you can refer the matter to the RTB for dispute resolution
4. You may also contact your local authority's Environmental Health Officer, as this could constitute a housing standards breach

We're CC'ing your management company on this email for their attention.

Warm regards,
Threshold Housing Advice
emails.body_plain:
Dear Grainne,

Thank you for contacting Threshold. We've reviewed the details you've provided regarding the heating failure at your apartment.

Under Section 12(1)(b) of the Residential Tenancies Act 2004, your landlord is obligated to maintain the property in a proper state of repair. The Housing (Standards for Rented Houses) Regulations 2019 specifically require that heating facilities are maintained in good repair and working order.

A heating failure lasting 3 days, particularly with a young infant in the property, is a serious breach. We recommend:

1. Send a written notice to your landlord/agent by email (for a paper trail) detailing the issue, the dates reported, and the lack of response
2. Set a reasonable deadline (48 hours given the circumstances)
3. If not resolved, you can refer the matter to the RTB for dispute resolution
4. You may also contact your local authority's Environmental Health Officer, as this could constitute a housing standards breach

We're CC'ing your management company on this email for their attention.

Warm regards,
Threshold Housing Advice

emails.subject: Re: Urgent Response - Heating Failure Emergency Action Plan
emails.body:
Dear Threshold Housing Advice,

We acknowledge receipt of your correspondence regarding the heating failure affecting Grainne and her infant child at our Reds Works property.

We take this matter extremely seriously and sincerely apologise for any delay in addressing this critical issue. The safety and welfare of our residents, particularly families with young children, is our highest priority.

**Immediate Actions Being Taken:**
1. Emergency contact with the tenant within 2 hours to assess current situation
2. Urgent heating engineer dispatch if any issues remain unresolved
3. Comprehensive review of maintenance response procedures
4. Alternative accommodation arrangements if required for child safety

**Our Commitment:**
We are committed to ensuring full compliance with our obligations under the Residential Tenancies Act 2004 and Housing Standards Regulations 2019. We will provide a full update on actions taken and resolution achieved within 24 hours.

We thank you for bringing this to our attention and for your advocacy on behalf of residents.

Urgent regards,

Reds Works Management Team
ManageCo Property Management

tasks.name: EMERGENCY: Contact Dublin Fire Brigade - Late Response
tasks.description:
**Contact DFB immediately about missed deadline**

**Contact:** Dublin Fire Brigade Fire Prevention Section
**Email:** fire.safety@dublinfire.ie
**Deadline missed:** March 27 (19 days overdue)

**Actions needed TODAY:**
1. Call DFB Fire Prevention Section immediately
2. Explain delay and apologize for missed deadline
3. Provide detailed remediation timeline with completion dates
4. Request guidance on enforcement implications
5. Schedule follow-up inspection once work completed
6. Confirm no interim enforcement action required

tasks.name: URGENT: Fix Emergency Lighting Car Park
tasks.description:
**Emergency lighting battery backup insufficient**

**Property:** Citynorth Quarter car park
**Issue:** Emergency lighting battery backup below minimum duration
**Deadline:** March 27 (MISSED)

**Actions needed:**
1. Contact emergency lighting contractor immediately
2. Test all car park emergency lighting units
3. Replace batteries or entire units as needed
4. Ensure minimum 3-hour backup duration compliance
5. Test and certify system functionality
6. Provide certification to DFB

tasks.name: URGENT: Install Bin Store Fire Suppression System
tasks.description:
**Major installation required - Fire suppression system**

**Property:** Citynorth Quarter bin store
**Issue:** No automatic fire suppression in enclosed bin store (code requirement)
**Deadline:** March 27 (MISSED)

**Actions needed:**
1. Contact fire suppression system contractors immediately
2. Get quotes and specifications for compliant system
3. Arrange planning/building approval if required
4. Schedule urgent installation (may take several days)
5. Arrange DFB inspection upon completion
6. Update fire safety certificate and documentation

tasks.name: EMERGENCY: Clear Fire Escape Stairwell Floor 4

5. Photograph cleared stairwell for DFB evidence
6. Schedule weekly compliance checks

tasks.name: EMERGENCY: Fix Fire Doors - Floors 3, 7, 10
